content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

November 26, 1990

John Palmer  
Post Office Box 11453  
Santa Rosa, CA 95401

Re: Your Request For Assistance  
Our File No. G-90-584

Dear Mr. Palmer:

We have received your inquiry concerning the campaign committee name of the union that represents the teaching staff of the Sonoma County office of education. You have indicated that the usage of the committee name, SCOE School Board Election Committee, on an invitation dated August 17, 1990, may be misleading to the voters because the Sonoma County Office of Education is commonly referred to as SCOE. You have asked whether the Commission regulates the use of names by campaign committees.

Your question does not concern a subject governed by the Political Reform Act of 1974 ("the Act"), found in Government Code §§ 81000-91015. The Commission's authority is limited to interpretation and enforcement of the Act. The Act regulates political campaign committees with respect to registration requirements, filing of campaign statements, disclosure of contributions and expenditures, identification of sponsors (when applicable), and identification of the sender on campaign mass mailings. However, the Act does not regulate the selection of a campaign committee's name in the circumstances you have described.1 Therefore, we cannot provide the assistance you have requested.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: Deanne Stone  
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:DS:aa

1 Section 84102(a) requires a sponsored committee to include the name of its sponsor.
